feat(agent): retry on different model for 502/503/429/5xx errors

When multiple models are configured in model_pool, retryable errors
(502, 503, 429, 500, 504, timeouts) now rotate to an untried model
instead of hammering the same failing endpoint.

- Add EngineError::RemoteStatus(u16, String) variant preserving HTTP status codes

feat(agent): add Anthropic Messages API thinking/extended thinking support

Seamlessly handles both OpenAI-compatible and Anthropic Messages API formats
with zero extra config required. Auto-detects Anthropic endpoints and translates
reasoning_effort → thinking_budget. Response parsing handles thinking blocks,
tool_use blocks, and Anthropic usage fields as fallbacks after OpenAI format.

- extract_assistant_content: Anthropic root content[] with type:"text" fallback
- extract_thinking_content: Anthropic type:"thinking" + OpenAI reasoning_content
- extract_usage: input_tokens/output_tokens fallback for Anthropic format
- parse_tool_calls: Anthropic type:"tool_use" content blocks fallback
- effective_thinking_payload: auto-translates reasoning_effort → budget_tokens
- Request structs: thinking + system fields, conditional Anthropic formatting
- 11 integration tests (unit + live OpenRouter), 178 types tests all passing
- No mutexes, no deadlocks, no panics — pure functions only

feat(agent): eval timeouts, spawn concurrency limiter, PageWaitStrategy, panic hardening

- Add eval_with_timeout() helper wrapping page.evaluate() with tokio timeout
- Replace all `let _ = page.evaluate(...)` with eval_with_timeout + proper
  error handling across engine loops (TTT, WAM, NEST, CIRCLE, WS) and
  action handlers (Press, KeyDown, KeyUp, Scroll*, GoBack, GoForward,
  Reload, Hover, Select, Focus, Blur, Evaluate)

  (DEFAULT_SPAWN_CONCURRENCY=5), wired into both run_spawn_pages_with_options
  and run_spawn_pages_with_factory

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(agent): action feedback, partial result preservation, and JS error capture

Three reliability improvements to the web automation engine:

1. Action feedback to LLM: execute_single_action now returns ActionOutcome
   with structured error messages (e.g., "selector not found: #btn").
   Failed action outcomes are injected as PREVIOUS ACTION RESULTS into the
   next round's prompt, enabling the model to self-correct.

2. Partial result preservation: LLM inference failures no longer propagate
   with `?`. Instead, accumulated extracted data, usage stats, and spawn
   pages from prior rounds are returned in AutomationResult with
   success=false, preventing data loss on transient API errors.

3. Evaluate JS error capture: JavaScript execution errors are now caught
   and surfaced through the action feedback system instead of being
   silently swallowed, giving the model visibility into its own JS mistakes.

All changes use stack-local Vec/String/Option only — no mutexes, no Arc,
no blocking. 207 tests pass with zero regressions.

style: fix rustfmt formatting in smart_vs_chrome tests
chore(release): 2.45.25

fix: use final redirect URL as base for relative link resolution (#364)

fix: use final_redirect_destination in set_url_parsed_direct

When a page follows a cross-domain redirect (e.g. article-1.eu → la-croix.com),
relative links from the response HTML must resolve against the redirect
destination, not the original URL. Otherwise parent_host_match fails to
reject foreign-domain links, flooding the crawl queue with phantom pages.

set_url_parsed_direct() now checks final_redirect_destination first.
Since all call sites already go through this method, the fix propagates
automatically — no changes needed elsewhere.

Tested on article-1.eu (WordPress site with a press-clipping page that
301-redirects to la-croix.com). Before: ~344 phantom depth=-1 pages from
la-croix.com navigation. After: zero.

fix(cache): proper HTTP staleness for Chrome-cached pages

Instead of blanket allow_stale bypass, fix the write path:

1. Override no-cache/no-store in policy headers with max-age=172800
   so CachePolicy.max_age() > 0 and Period(now-2d) works correctly
2. Use shared=false for CachePolicy (per-crawl cache, not shared proxy)
   so Set-Cookie doesn't force max_age=0
3. Respect server's last-modified/expires/max-age when present
   (no override needed — heuristic works naturally)

This gives real HTTP-semantics-based staleness:
- Entries with last-modified use the 10% heuristic (e.g. 109 days)
- Entries with explicit max-age use that value
- Entries with no-cache/no-store get 2-day fallback max-age
- Period(now-2d) trick correctly computes freshness in all cases

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(cache): Period policy bypasses HTTP is_stale for Chrome-rendered pages

Chrome-rendered pages typically have Cache-Control: no-cache/no-store or
Set-Cookie headers. The http-cache-semantics CachePolicy computes max_age=0
for these, making is_stale() always return true — even with Period policy.

When BasicCachePolicy::Period is active, treat entries as allow_stale since
the user explicitly opted into time-based caching that overrides HTTP semantics.

Added tests verifying no-cache and no-store responses are served from cache
with Period policy.

fix(cache): enable cache_chrome_hybrid_mem feature for Chrome cache writes

The cache_chrome_hybrid_mem feature was silently broken — put_hybrid_cache
and cache_chrome_response compiled as no-ops because their cfg gates only
checked for cache_chrome_hybrid (file-based), not cache_chrome_hybrid_mem
(in-memory). This caused Chrome-crawled pages to never be written to cache,
making every subsequent Chrome request re-launch the browser instead of
serving from cache. HTTP/smart mode was unaffected because http-cache-reqwest
middleware handles caching through a different path.

Changes:
- Update all cfg gates from `cache_chrome_hybrid` to
  `any(cache_chrome_hybrid, cache_chrome_hybrid_mem)`
- Fix swapped headers in put_hybrid_cache (response headers were used for
  request CachePolicy and vice versa, causing entries to always be stale)
- Consolidate duplicate cache_chrome_response variants into one
- Add _mem-specific tests for shortcircuit and multi-page cache phase
- Fix existing tests to put cache-control in response_headers

fix: add no_sandbox() to headless BrowserConfig (#354)

fix: add no_sandbox() to headless BrowserConfig and log Browser::launch errors

The non-chrome_headed get_browser_config() calls .disable_default_args() which
prevents chromiumoxide from adding its default --no-sandbox flag. With the
real_browser feature (enabled by basic), CHROME_ARGS is the 24-arg variant
that does not include --no-sandbox. This causes Chrome to crash with:
'Running as root without --no-sandbox is not supported' in Docker/CI.

The chrome_headed variant already has .no_sandbox() — this aligns the headless
config to match.

Also replace .ok() with explicit error logging in setup_browser_configuration()
so Browser::launch failures are visible instead of silently swallowed.

commit.message:
fix: Chrome mode honors wait_for config for networkIdle before HTML extraction

The !run_events path in fetch_page_html_chrome_base skipped page_wait entirely,
so deferred JS/XHR content could be missed. Now applies wait_for config (e.g.
idle_network0) before outer_html_bytes(), matching smart mode behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: smart mode lifecycle waiting — match Chrome coverage without re-fetch

goto_with_html_once now waits for load + networkIdle after Fetch.fulfillRequest,
matching http_future behavior in the normal Chrome path. should_upgrade triggers
on external scripts regardless of link count, closing the smart-vs-chrome gap.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: auto-retry www. URLs on SSL protocol errors — strip www prefix

When Chrome navigation fails with net::ERR_SSL_PROTOCOL_ERROR,
ERR_CERT_COMMON_NAME_INVALID, or ERR_CERT_AUTHORITY_INVALID on a www.
URL, automatically retry without the www. prefix (e.g.
www.docs.github.com → docs.github.com). Also retries in the reqwest
path when TLS handshake fails on www. URLs. Zero overhead on success
path — checks only run after error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: reject empty HTML from all cache and seeded resource paths

Extends empty-HTML invalidation to cover seeded_resource bypass in
fetch_page_html_base and build_seed_page. Ensures empty shell HTML
never flows through the subscription channel from any cache path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: invalidate empty HTML shell responses on cache read path

Cache reads returning `<html><head></head><body></body></html>` are now
treated as cache misses so the crawl continues instead of short-circuiting
with an empty page.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: deferred Chrome — cache-only crawl phase before browser launch

Multi-page Chrome/HTTP crawls now process cached pages first via
crawl_cache_phase(). Links are followed through cache; only cache
misses trigger browser/HTTP. If everything is cached, Chrome never
starts. No mutexes, no shared state — lock-free cache lookups only.

6 new tests, all passing. Zero regressions on existing cache tests.

Bump all workspace crates to v2.45.9.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: cache-first fast path — skip browser/HTTP when cache has data

When cache_skip_browser is enabled and cache has the data, skip Chrome
launch, tab creation, HTTP client setup, and robots.txt fetch entirely.
Single-page crawls return in ~5-50ms instead of 1-3s. Multi-page crawls
skip tab creation per-page on cache hit. No locks, no deadlocks, zero
regressions on cache miss paths.

Add a new `hedge` feature flag that enables speculative duplicate requests
on different proxies when a primary request is slow. After a configurable

and the loser is cancelled via drop.

perf(website): use take() instead of clone() for subdomain base URL

Avoids cloning the parsed Url when constructing page_base for link
resolution. The moved-out value is recalculated inside links() via
set_url_parsed_direct_empty() which is a no-alloc check when base
is already set, or a single parse when None — same net cost minus
the String clone overhead.

fix(website): add tests for subdomain relative URL resolution, bump v2.44.41

Add unit and integration tests verifying that relative links on subdomain
pages resolve against the page's own host instead of the crawl origin.
Tests cover validate_link with subdomain base, same-domain regression,
and full page.links() integration with subdomain HTML.

fix(website): use page's own URL for relative link resolution on subdomains

When crawling with subdomains enabled, relative links (e.g. `href="/path"`)
on subdomain pages were resolved against the original crawl domain instead
of the current page's host.

For example, crawling `www.example.com` with subdomains, a page on
`sub.example.com` with `<a href="/about">` would resolve to
`www.example.com/about` instead of `sub.example.com/about`, causing
phantom 404s and wasted crawl requests.

The fix uses `page.set_url_parsed_direct()` to set the base URL from the
page's own URL (`page.url`, which stores the originally queued URL, not
any redirect destination) instead of overriding it with the original crawl
domain. Domain validation is unaffected as it uses separate selector
parameters.

feat(agent): integrate llm_models_spider v0.1.9 with smart model selection

Add ModelSelector for intelligent multi-model routing based on arena
rankings, pricing, and capabilities. Users can pass in available models
and get automatic scoring/selection by task type (BestQuality,
CheapestFirst, LargestContext, ValueOptimal). Supports custom priority
overrides, vision/audio requirement filtering, and multi-agent dispatch
for assigning different models to different sub-tasks. Also adds
auto_policy() to build ModelPolicy from available models using arena
data. Re-exports all new llm_models_spider APIs (model_profile,
arena_rank, supports_video, supports_pdf, MODEL_INFO).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: add missing feature flags, Spider Cloud example, and integration docs

Add comprehensive feature flag documentation covering spider_cloud, agent,
search, webdriver, wreq, adblock, caching, Chrome remote cache, tracing,
simd, and more. Add Spider Cloud and Chrome rendering code examples.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(agent): move all skill content to spider_skills crate

- Remove all inline skill content constants from spider_agent
- Load 110 skills from spider_skills::web_challenges::registry()
- Keep only pre_evaluate JS constants in spider_agent (engine-specific)
- Add pre_evaluate overlays via get_mut after loading registry
- Require spider_skills 0.1.7 with web_challenges feature
- Bump all crates to v2.44.39

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(agent): add L15-L48 skills, NEST/CIRCLE engine loops, haiku benchmark

- Add 34 new skills covering all 48 challenge levels (parking, 3D, draw
  circle, push/drag, dark mode, inkblot, crafting, counting, panorama,
  eye chart, creative, networking, trading, sliding puzzle, traffic,
  rhythm, brands, math, shuffle, match-3, imposters, decisions, faces,
  slots, dig/find, reverse turing, assembly, chess, find person, floors,
  bell pattern, final creative)
- NEST engine loop: auto-detects image overlap with leaf boxes, clicks
  via CDP, handles recursive subdivision in tight loop
- CIRCLE engine loop: draws 36-point circular mouse path via CDP drag
- Math pre_evaluate: extracts and solves expressions from DOM
- Sliding puzzle pre_evaluate: reads tile positions for solver
- Add not_a_robot_haiku.rs example for cheap model benchmarking

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add should_use_chrome_ai to stub RemoteMultimodalConfigs

The stub struct (used when agent feature is not enabled) was missing
the use_chrome_ai field and should_use_chrome_ai() method, causing
compilation errors with chrome feature alone.

feat(agent): add L8-L14 skills, WAM engine loop, and Chrome AI improvements

Add built-in skills for license plate, nested grid, whack-a-mole, find Waldo,
chihuahua/muffin, reverse selection, and affirmations challenges. Implement
whack-a-mole engine loop for fast repeated click detection. Improve Chrome AI
element probe with better filtering and session invalidation on stuck rounds.

feat: extraction-only mode optimization for single-round data extraction

Auto-detect extraction-only mode (extra_ai_data=true, max_rounds<=1) and optimize:
- Use focused EXTRACTION_ONLY_SYSTEM_PROMPT (~400 chars) instead of full automation prompt (~3000 chars)
- Skip screenshots for extraction-only calls (saves ~35k tokens per call)
- Recover extracted data from Fill steps when weak models emit actions instead of data
- Treat empty extracted: {} as missing for fallback recovery
- Inject extraction schema/prompt/relevance into browser.rs infer_plan_once

Zero impact on multi-round automation (max_rounds > 1). Honors include_screenshot=Some(true) override.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(agent): add URL-level relevance pre-filter for crawling, bump to v2.44.18

Add url_prefilter option that classifies URLs via a cheap LLM text model
call BEFORE fetching them. URLs deemed irrelevant are never fetched,
saving both HTTP request time and LLM extraction costs. Results are
cached to avoid re-classifying the same URL patterns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: fast-path trie protocol detection, remove redundant char boundary check

- Trie path_start: check common https:// and http:// prefixes first
  before falling back to generic find("://") scan
- Trie path_start: use byte iterator for '/' search after scheme (avoids
  str::find overhead)
- handle_base: remove redundant is_char_boundary check for ASCII protocols
- Mark Entry::applies_to as #[cfg(test)] to silence dead code warning

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: optimize trie, robot parser, and URL preparation hot paths, bump to v2.44.17

- Trie insert: use hashbrown entry_ref to avoid String alloc on existing keys
- Robot parser: hoist to_lowercase out of per-entry loops in can_fetch/crawl_delay/req_rate
- prepare_url: replace char_indices().nth() with direct byte indexing + pre-allocated String
- Agent skills: streamline image grid skill for faster solving
- Add 15 new tests covering all optimized code paths

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(agent): add relevance gate for remote multimodal crawling

Add `relevance_gate` config that instructs the LLM to return a `relevant`
field in its JSON response. Irrelevant pages get their wildcard budget
credits refunded so the crawler finds more relevant content.

- Add `relevance_gate: bool` and `relevance_prompt: Option<String>` to
  RemoteMultimodalConfig with builder methods
- Add atomic `relevance_credits` counter to RemoteMultimodalConfigs
- Add `relevant: Option<bool>` to AutomationResult and AutomationResults
- Extend system prompt with relevance gate instructions when enabled
- Parse `relevant` field from LLM responses in engine and browser paths
- Add `restore_wildcard_budget()` to website.rs
- Drain relevance credits in dequeue() to restore wildcard budget
- Bump versions to v2.44.16

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: optimize core pipeline hot paths, add benchmarks, bump to v2.44.15

Trie: eliminate normalize_path String allocation — insert 49% faster, search 60-70% faster.
Robot parser: remove redundant to_lowercase() calls and Vec alloc — parse 50% faster.
URL handling: simplify protocol detection, replace char_indices with direct slice check.
HTML cleaning: merge element selectors (base 11→3, slim 17→4 handlers).
Add criterion micro-benchmarks for 7 groups (25 functions) across hot-path utilities.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

releases.name: v2.45.28
releases.body:
### Agent Hardening

- Cap LLM-controlled durations (Wait, ClickHold, SetViewport, OpenPage)
- Add `js_escape()` for safe JS string interpolation in action handlers
- Wrap `Navigate` and screenshot calls with timeouts
- Use `PageWaitStrategy::Load` for `WaitForNavigation` instead of fixed sleep
- Replace `eval_with_timeout` for Fill/Type/Clear actions with error propagation
- Improve semaphore and logging diagnostics on error paths

releases.name: v2.45.24
releases.body:
## What's New

### Performance
- **Cache-first fast path** — skip browser/HTTP entirely when cache has data (~5-50ms vs 1-3s)
- **Deferred Chrome** — process multi-page crawls from cache before launching a browser
- **Work-stealing (hedged requests)** — parallel retry for slow crawl requests
- **io_uring** — StreamingWriter for high-throughput file I/O on Linux

### Agent
- **Per-round model pool routing** — route cheap rounds to fast models, complex rounds to capable ones
- **Comprehensive router tests** — 30+ multi-LLM reliability tests

### Fixes
- Chrome mode honors `wait_for` config (e.g. `idle_network0`) before HTML extraction
- Smart mode lifecycle waiting matches Chrome coverage without re-fetch
- Auto-retry www. URLs on SSL protocol errors
- Reject empty HTML from all cache paths
- Default `no_sandbox()` for headless Chrome (#354)

### CLI
- `--wait-for` flag for page load strategies (#352)

### Housekeeping
- Clippy warnings and formatting cleanup across workspace
- Rewritten README, CONTRIBUTING.md, CHANGELOG backfill
- Issue/PR templates, security policy, CI workflows (lint, audit, release)

releases.name: v2.45.20
releases.body:
## What's New

### Relevance Gate for Remote Multimodal Crawling

Added a `relevance_gate` config that instructs the LLM to return a `"relevant": true|false` field in its JSON response. When a page is deemed irrelevant, its wildcard budget credit is refunded so the crawler discovers more relevant content.

**New config fields:**
- `relevance_gate: bool` — enables the feature
- `relevance_prompt: Option<String>` — optional custom relevance criteria

**How it works:**
1. When enabled, the system prompt instructs the LLM to include `"relevant": true|false`
2. If the model returns `false`, a budget credit is atomically accumulated
3. Credits are drained in the crawl loop to restore the wildcard budget
4. Default fallback is `true` (assume relevant) if the model omits the field

**Example:**
```rust
let cfgs = RemoteMultimodalConfigs::new(api_url, model)
    .with_relevance_gate(Some("Only pages about Rust programming".into()));
```

### Full Changelog
- feat(agent): add `relevance_gate` and `relevance_prompt` to `RemoteMultimodalConfig`
- feat(agent): add atomic `relevance_credits` counter to `RemoteMultimodalConfigs`

readme_html:
Spider

Website | Guides | API Docs | Examples | Discord

A high-performance web crawler and scraper for Rust. 200-1000x faster than popular alternatives, with HTTP, headless Chrome, and WebDriver rendering in a single library.

  • Crawl 100k+ pages in minutes on a single machine. See benchmarks.
  • HTTP, Chrome CDP, WebDriver, and AI automation in one dependency.
  • Production-ready with caching, proxy rotation, anti-bot bypass, and distributed crawling. Feature-gated so you only compile what you use.

Quick Start

Command Line

cargo install spider_cli
spider --url https://example.com

Rust

[dependencies]
spider = "2"

use spider::tokio;
use spider::website::Website;

#[tokio::main]
async fn main() {
    let mut website = Website::new("https://example.com");
    website.crawl().await;
    println!("Pages found: {}", website.get_links().len());
}

Streaming

Process each page the moment it's crawled, not after:

use spider::tokio;
use spider::website::Website;

#[tokio::main]
async fn main() {
    let mut website = Website::new("https://example.com");
    let mut rx = website.subscribe(0).unwrap();

    tokio::spawn(async move {
        while let Ok(page) = rx.recv().await {
            println!("- {}", page.get_url());
        }
    });

    website.crawl().await;
    website.unsubscribe();
}

Headless Chrome

Add one feature flag to render JavaScript-heavy pages:

[dependencies]
spider = { version = "2", features = ["chrome"] }

use spider::features::chrome_common::RequestInterceptConfiguration;
use spider::website::Website;

#[tokio::main]
async fn main() {
    let mut website = Website::new("https://example.com")
        .with_chrome_intercept(RequestInterceptConfiguration::new(true))
        .with_stealth(true)
        .build()
        .unwrap();

    website.crawl().await;
}

Also supports WebDriver (Selenium Grid, remote browsers) and AI-driven automation. See examples for more.

Benchmarks

Crawling 185 pages (source, 10 samples averaged):

Apple M1 Max (10-core, 64 GB RAM):

Crawler Language Time vs Spider
spider Rust 73 ms baseline
node-crawler JavaScript 15 s 205x slower
colly Go 32 s 438x slower
wget C 70 s 959x slower

Linux (2-core, 7 GB RAM):

Crawler Language Time vs Spider
spider Rust 50 ms baseline
node-crawler JavaScript 3.4 s 68x slower
colly Go 30 s 600x slower
wget C 60 s 1200x slower

The gap grows with site size. Spider handles 100k+ pages in minutes where other crawlers take hours. This comes from Rust's async runtime (tokio), lock-free data structures, and optional io_uring on Linux. Full details

Why Spider?

Most crawlers force a choice between fast HTTP-only or slow-but-flexible browser automation. Spider supports both, and you can mix them in the same crawl.

Supports HTTP, Chrome, and WebDriver. Switch rendering modes with a feature flag. Use HTTP for speed, Chrome CDP for JavaScript-heavy pages, and WebDriver for Selenium Grid or cross-browser testing.

Only compile what you use. Every optional capability (Chrome, caching, proxies, AI) lives behind a Cargo feature flag. A minimal spider = "2" stays lean.

Built for production. Caching (memory, disk, hybrid), proxy rotation, anti-bot fingerprinting, ad blocking, depth budgets, cron scheduling, and distributed workers. All of this has been hardened through Spider Cloud.

AI automation included. spider_agent adds multimodal LLM-driven automation: navigate pages, fill forms, solve challenges, and extract structured data with OpenAI or any compatible API.

Features

  • Concurrent and streaming crawls with backpressure
  • Decentralized crawling for horizontal scaling
  • Caching: memory, disk (SQLite), or hybrid Chrome cache
  • Proxy support with rotation
  • Cron job scheduling
  • Depth budgeting, blacklisting, whitelisting
  • Smart mode that auto-detects JS-rendered content and upgrades to Chrome
  • Chrome DevTools Protocol: headless or headed, stealth mode, screenshots, request interception
  • WebDriver: Selenium Grid, remote browsers, cross-browser testing
  • AI-powered challenge solving (deterministic + Chrome built-in AI)
  • Anti-bot fingerprinting, ad blocking, firewall
  • HTML transformations (Markdown, text, structured extraction)
  • CSS/XPath scraping with spider_utils
  • OpenAI and Gemini integration for content analysis
  • spider_agent: concurrent-safe multimodal web automation agent
  • Multiple LLM providers (OpenAI, any OpenAI-compatible API, Chrome built-in AI)
  • Web research with search providers (Serper, Brave, Bing, Tavily)
  • 110 built-in automation skills for web challenges

Spider Cloud

For managed proxy rotation, anti-bot bypass, and CAPTCHA handling, Spider Cloud plugs in with one line:

let mut website = Website::new("https://protected-site.com")
    .with_spider_cloud("your-api-key")  // enable with features = ["spider_cloud"]
    .build()
    .unwrap();
Mode Strategy Best For
Proxy (default) All traffic through Spider Cloud proxy General crawling with IP rotation
Smart (recommended) Proxy + auto-fallback on bot detection Production (speed + reliability)
Fallback Direct first, API on failure Cost-efficient, most sites work without help
Unblocker All requests through unblocker Aggressive bot protection

Free credits on signup. Get started at spider.cloud

Get Spider

Package Language Install
spider Rust cargo add spider
spider_cli CLI cargo install spider_cli
spider-nodejs Node.js npm i @spider-rs/spider-rs
spider-py Python pip install spider_rs
spider_agent Rust cargo add spider --features agent

Cloud and Remote

Package Description
Spider Cloud Managed crawling infrastructure, no setup needed
spider-clients SDKs for Spider Cloud in multiple languages
spider-browser Remote access to Spider's Rust browser
